title: Emma Walsh
content:
People.ContactEmail ewalsh@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2024
Emma is an associate in our Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group in Vancouver. She is building a broad and diversified civil litigation practice, with a particular interest in class actions, public and administrative law, and complex commercial disputes. She is also a contributing author to the sixth edition of Defending Class Actions in Canada , published by LexisNexis.
Emma earned her Juris Doctor and Bachelor of Civil Law degrees from McGill University. She graduated on the Dean’s Honour list and received numerous academic awards and scholarships, including recognition as the top graduate in public international law courses. Emma gained well-rounded experiences in law school by working at three legal clinics, editing for a law journal, representing students to the law faculty, and co-founding an advocacy organization to aid political prisoners abroad. She also competed with McGill’s Laskin Moot team, finishing second overall.
Prior to law school, Emma obtained her Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Political Science and Honours in Human Rights at St. Thomas University in New Brunswick. While there, she competed in moot court competitions across the United States and Europe, winning several top oralist and first place team awards. She was also elected to represent more than 350,000 post-secondary students in Canada to the federal government
Emma joins McCarthy Tétrault as an associate. During law school, she summered at a litigation boutique in Toronto. Following law school, she clerked for a justice at the Federal Court of Appeal. There, she placed first and was awarded top oralist in the Federal Courts’ internal moot. Emma was called to the bar of British Columbia and bar of Ontario in 2024.
